purpose.claim: Supabase is a Firebase alternative built on Postgres, providing a Postgres development platform with features like hosted Postgres Database, Authentication and Authorization, Auto-generated APIs, Functions, File Storage, and AI + Vector/Embeddings Toolkit.

mainWorkflows.claim: Users can sign up and log in using the GoTrue authentication API, which simplifies user session management.
mainWorkflows.name: User Authentication

mainWorkflows.claim: Supabase uses Postgres as its database, allowing for reliable and scalable data storage and retrieval.
mainWorkflows.name: Data Storage and Retrieval

dataModel.claim: Supabase's data model is based on Postgres, with features like tables, rows, and columns, and supports advanced data types like JSON and arrays.

integrations.claim: Supabase uses Postgres as its primary database, providing a scalable and reliable data storage solution.

integrations.claim: Supabase uses GoTrue for authentication and authorization, providing a simple and secure way to manage user sessions.

architecturePattern.claim: Supabase follows a microservices architecture pattern, with a Kong API gateway sitting in front of multiple services like GoTrue, PostgREST, Realtime, Storage, and Functions.

claims.claim: Supabase is built using a variety of open-source tools and technologies, including Postgres, GoTrue, and Kong.
Supabase provides a range of features and tools for building and deploying applications, including auto-generated APIs, functions, and file storage.
Supabase has a strong focus on security and reliability, with features like authentication and authorization, and a scalable and reliable database solution.

decisions.title: Adopt Postgres as the primary database
decisions.description: The team chose Postgres as the primary database for Supabase due to its reliability and scalability.
decisions.assessment: Sound decision, given Postgres's reputation for reliability and scalability.

decisions.title: Implement Row-Level Security (RLS) using Postgres
decisions.description: The team implemented RLS using Postgres to ensure secure data access and management.
decisions.assessment: Good decision, as RLS provides an additional layer of security for sensitive data.

decisions.title: Use GoTrue for authentication and authorization
decisions.description: The team chose GoTrue for authentication and authorization due to its simplicity and security features.
decisions.assessment: Reasonable decision, considering GoTrue's ease of use and security features.

decisions.title: Implement auto-generated APIs and functions
decisions.description: The team implemented auto-generated APIs and functions to simplify development and improve productivity.
decisions.assessment: Good decision, as auto-generated APIs and functions can reduce development time and improve consistency.

decisions.title: Use a microservices architecture pattern
decisions.description: The team adopted a microservices architecture pattern to improve scalability and maintainability.
decisions.assessment: Sound decision, given the benefits of microservices architecture in terms of scalability and maintainability.

decisions.title: Add healthcheck to PostgREST service

decisions.assessment: Good decision, as healthchecks can help identify issues and improve overall system reliability.

decisions.assessment: Good decision, as improved redirect validation can help prevent security issues.

decisions.description: Django supports various databases, including PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the relational model fits invoicing and reporting.

decisions.title: Improved security standards in documentation

decisions.assessment: Good practice: security standards are well-documented.

decisions.title: Fixed bug in CountsDict.__init__()
decisions.description: The bug was fixed by changing *kwargs to **kwargs in the call to super().__init__()
decisions.assessment: Sound: the fix correctly forwards keyword arguments to dict.__init__()

decisions.title: Added helper to properly split header values
decisions.description: The helper was added to extract header values and strip whitespace.
decisions.assessment: Good practice: the helper improves header value parsing.

decisions.title: Reduced false positives in strings during collectstatic
decisions.description: The change expands the is_in_comment concept to ignore strings for css and js.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the change reduces false positives.

decisions.title: Added --using option to sendtestemail command
decisions.description: The option allows testing non-default mailer configurations.
decisions.assessment: Good practice: the option improves mailer configuration testing.

decisions.title: Clarified database cache culling behavior in docs
decisions.description: The documentation was updated to reflect the correct culling behavior.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the documentation accurately reflects the culling behavior.

decisions.title: Skipped backends not implementing get_user() in force_login()
decisions.description: The change skips backends that do not implement get_user() to prevent incorrect selection.
decisions.assessment: Good practice: the change prevents incorrect backend selection.

decisions.title: Moved django_file_prefixes() to django.utils.warnings
decisions.description: The function was moved to make it a public API.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the move makes the function a public API.

decisions.title: Made HttpRequest.body handle malformed CONTENT_LENGTH
decisions.description: The change handles malformed CONTENT_LENGTH values to prevent errors.
decisions.assessment: Good practice: the change improves error handling.

decisions.title: Replaced substring check on Cache-control directives
decisions.description: The change replaces the substring check to avoid false positives.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the change improves Cache-control directive parsing.

purpose.claim: Ruby on Rails is a web application framework that includes everything needed to create database-backed web applications according to the Model-View-Controller (MVC) pattern.

mainWorkflows.claim: The framework handles incoming HTTP requests and provides a suitable response, usually returning HTML, but can also generate XML, JSON, PDFs, mobile-specific views, and more.
mainWorkflows.name: Request/Response Cycle

dataModel.claim: The framework uses the Active Record pattern to interact with the database, and the Model layer represents the domain model, encapsulating business logic specific to the application.

integrations.claim: The framework supports various databases, including MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQLite, through the use of Active Record.
integrations.name: Database

architecturePattern.claim: The framework follows the Model-View-Controller (MVC) pattern, dividing the application into three layers: Model, View, and Controller, each with a specific responsibility.

claims.claim: The framework is written in Ruby.
The framework uses the Ruby on Rails framework.
The framework has a large community and is widely used.

decisions.title: Adopt Ractor-safe design for Active Record
decisions.description: Make Active Record more Ractor-safe by freezing and constantizing various modules and classes.
decisions.assessment: Sound: Ractor safety is essential for concurrent programming in Rails.

decisions.title: Use shims for Ractor shareability methods
decisions.description: Add shims for Ractor shareability methods to avoid version checks in the codebase.
decisions.assessment: Sound: shims simplify the code and reduce version-related issues.

decisions.title: Make some constants Ractor-safe
decisions.description: Freeze some constants to make them Ractor-safe.
decisions.assessment: Sound: Ractor safety is essential for concurrent programming in Rails.

decisions.title: Make some lambdas in constants shareable
decisions.description: Use the shareability shim to make lambdas in constants shareable on Ruby 4.0+.
decisions.assessment: Sound: shareable lambdas improve Ractor safety in Rails.

decisions.title: Ractor-safe readonly attributes
decisions.description: Make readonly attributes Ractor-safe by freezing them by default.
decisions.assessment: Sound: Ractor safety is essential for concurrent programming in Rails.

decisions.title: Support a single composite primary key id in update/update!
decisions.description: Update the class-level update/update! methods to support a single composite primary key id.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the update/update! methods should handle composite primary keys correctly.

decisions.title: Keep the Redis listener alive when the last channel is unsubscribed
decisions.description: Restore the sentinel subscription to keep the Redis listener alive when the last channel is unsubscribed.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the Redis listener should remain alive even when the last channel is unsubscribed.

decisions.title: Shut down the heartbeat timer on server restart
decisions.description: Shut down and clear the heartbeat timer on server restart to prevent thread leaks.
decisions.assessment: Sound: shutting down the heartbeat timer prevents thread leaks and improves server restart behavior.

decisions.title: Raise ChannelNotFound for a subscribe command without a channel
decisions.description: Raise a ChannelNotFound error when a subscribe command is missing a channel.
decisions.assessment: Sound: raising a ChannelNotFound error improves error handling in Action Cable.

decisions.title: Keep pending subscribe confirmations across a Redis reconnect
decisions.description: Keep pending subscribe confirmations across a Redis reconnect to prevent lost confirmations.
decisions.assessment: Sound: keeping pending subscribe confirmations improves the reliability of Action Cable.

decisions.title: Respect an explicit id: nil in the Redis cable config
decisions.description: Respect an explicit id: nil in the Redis cable config to allow for custom connection naming.
decisions.assessment: Sound: respecting an explicit id: nil improves the flexibility of Action Cable's Redis configuration.

decisions.title: Coerce stream name to String in the test adapter accessors
decisions.description: Coerce the stream name to a String in the test adapter accessors to ensure correct channel lookup.
decisions.assessment: Sound: coercing the stream name to a String improves the reliability of Action Cable's test adapter.

decisions.title: Coerce broadcasting to String in Channel#stop_stream_from
decisions.description: Coerce the broadcasting to a String in Channel#stop_stream_from to ensure correct channel lookup.
decisions.assessment: Sound: coercing the broadcasting to a String improves the reliability of Action Cable's channel management.

decisions.title: Iterate a snapshot of the connections in Server#each_connection
decisions.description: Iterate a snapshot of the connections in Server#each_connection to prevent concurrency issues.
decisions.assessment: Sound: iterating a snapshot of connections improves the reliability of Action Cable's server management.

decisions.title: Don't mutate the caller's string in Inflector#transliterate
decisions.description: Don't mutate the caller's string in Inflector#transliterate to prevent unexpected side effects.
decisions.assessment: Sound: not mutating the caller's string improves the reliability and predictability of Inflector#transliterate.

decisions.description: GORM follows a modular architecture pattern with separate modules for different components.
decisions.context: GORM's architecture is designed to be modular and scalable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the modular architecture fits the needs of a large and complex ORM library.

decisions.title: Using a Schema-Based Data Model
decisions.description: GORM uses a schema-based data model to define the structure of the data.
decisions.context: GORM's data model is designed to be flexible and adaptable to different database schemas.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the schema-based data model provides a clear and consistent way of defining data structures.

decisions.title: Supporting Various Database Operations
decisions.description: GORM supports various database operations such as create, read, update, and delete.
decisions.context: GORM's database operations are designed to be efficient and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the support for various database operations makes GORM a versatile and useful library.

decisions.title: Improving Concurrency with RWMutex
decisions.description: GORM uses RWMutex to improve concurrency in its internal LRU cache.
decisions.context: GORM's internal LRU cache is designed to be thread-safe and efficient.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the use of RWMutex improves the performance and reliability of GORM's internal LRU cache.

decisions.title: Fixing Nil Pointer Panic in Serializer
decisions.description: GORM fixes a nil pointer panic in its serializer to prevent crashes.
decisions.context: GORM's serializer is designed to be robust and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the fix prevents crashes and improves the overall reliability of GORM.

decisions.title: Adding Unit Test for Chainable Query Methods
decisions.description: GORM adds a unit test to verify that chainable query methods mutate the statement correctly.
decisions.context: GORM's query methods are designed to be flexible and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the unit test improves the confidence in GORM's query methods.

decisions.title: Fixing Close Connection After Ping Failure
decisions.description: GORM fixes a bug where the connection is not closed after a ping failure, preventing goroutine leaks.
decisions.context: GORM's connection management is designed to be robust and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the fix prevents goroutine leaks and improves the overall reliability of GORM.

decisions.title: Updating Func for Generics
decisions.description: GORM updates its func for generics to fix a bug where the wrong type is raised at runtime.
decisions.context: GORM's generics support is designed to be flexible and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the update fixes a bug and improves the overall reliability of GORM's generics support.

decisions.title: Applying QueryFields to RETURNING Clauses
decisions.description: GORM applies QueryFields to RETURNING clauses to generate explicit column lists from the model schema.
decisions.context: GORM's QueryFields support is designed to be flexible and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the application of QueryFields to RETURNING clauses improves the overall reliability and flexibility of GORM.

decisions.title: Using Pgroll for Automigration
decisions.description: GORM considers using pgroll for automigration to reduce possible downtimes or conflicts.
decisions.context: GORM's automigration support is designed to be robust and reliable.
decisions.assessment: Sound: the use of pgroll for automigration improves the overall reliability and flexibility of GORM.
